column1: # Plant Hybrid ASE Pipeline — Sample Sheet
# One row per sample. Tab-separated.
# Lines starting with # are comments and are ignored.
# COLUMNS:
#   sample_id    Unique sample identifier. Used as wildcard {sample}
#                throughout the pipeline. No spaces or special characters.
#   data_type    Sequencing assay. Must be one of:
#                  RNA-seq | ATAC-seq | ChIP-seq | MOA-seq
#                Controls which aligner and WASP method are used.
#   vcf_sample   Sample name as it appears in the VCF column header.
#                Single-sample VCF (e.g. parental alignment VCF):
#                  Set this to the one VCF sample name for EVERY row.
#                  Example: all rows have vcf_sample = "Oh43"
#                Multi-sample VCF (e.g. GATK joint genotyping):
#                  Set this to the matching VCF column name per row.
#                  Example: sample B73xOh43_rep1 → vcf_sample = "B73xOh43_rep1"
#   R1           Path to raw Read 1 FASTQ (gzipped).
#                Required if start_from: fastq. Otherwise set to NA.
#   R2           Path to raw Read 2 FASTQ (gzipped).
#                Required if start_from: fastq. Otherwise set to NA.
#   trimmed_R1   Path to trimmed Read 1 FASTQ (gzipped).
#                Required if start_from: trimmed. Otherwise set to NA.
#                If start_from: fastq, pipeline writes trimmed FASTQs here
#                automatically — leave as NA and pipeline fills it in.
#   trimmed_R2   Path to trimmed Read 2 FASTQ (gzipped).
#                Same rules as trimmed_R1.
#   bam          Path to aligned BAM (sorted, indexed).
#                Required if start_from: bam. Otherwise set to NA.
#   wasp_bam     Path to WASP-filtered BAM (sorted, indexed).
#                Required if start_from: wasp_bam. Otherwise set to NA.
# Fill only the columns relevant to your start_from setting.
# Set all other path columns to NA.
#   start_from: fastq     → fill: sample_id, data_type, vcf_sample, R1, R2
#   start_from: trimmed   → fill: sample_id, data_type, vcf_sample, trimmed_R1, trimmed_R2
#   start_from: bam       → fill: sample_id, data_type, vcf_sample, bam
#   start_from: wasp_bam  → fill: sample_id, data_type, vcf_sample, wasp_bam
#   start_from: counts    → fill: sample_id, data_type, vcf_sample
# EXAMPLE A — Single-sample VCF (parental alignment VCF)
# All hybrid samples share the same vcf_sample name "Oh43"
# (the Parent B sample column in the VCF)
# sample_id
# B73xOh43_RNA_rep1
# B73xOh43_RNA_rep2
# B73xOh43_ATAC_rep1
# EXAMPLE B — Multi-sample VCF (GATK joint genotyping)
# Each sample has its own genotype column in the VCF
# sample_id
# B73xOh43_RNA_rep1
# B73xOh43_RNA_rep2
# B73xOh43_ATAC_rep1
# DELETE the comment lines above and fill in your samples below
sample_id
